metadata.description: General non-negotiation clarity_and_directness pairs for probing only. Tests whether the clarity_and_directness vector generalises beyond negotiation contexts to everyday situations requiring clear and direct communication.
metadata.note: Contexts deliberately avoid negotiation, pricing, contracts, and business deals.

dimensions.name: Clarity and Directness
dimensions.description: Communicating in clear, specific, unambiguous terms; making explicit statements rather than hinting or being vague

pairs.context: You want to decline a social invitation from an acquaintance.
pairs.positive: Thanks for the invite. I'm not going to be able to make it and I won't be able to come to the next couple of events either — I need to protect my weekends at the moment.
pairs.negative: I'd love to but things have been a bit chaotic lately and I'm not sure what my schedule looks like. I'll try to see if I can make it work.

pairs.context: A teacher needs a student to redo part of their assignment before it can be marked.
pairs.positive: Before this can be assessed, you need to rewrite the conclusion. It doesn't address the question. Resubmit it by Thursday and I'll mark it then.
pairs.negative: The conclusion could perhaps be developed a bit more to really bring out what you were arguing. Take another look at it when you can.

pairs.context: A flatmate keeps leaving dishes in the sink and you have raised it indirectly several times.
pairs.positive: I need to say this directly: please wash your dishes the same day you use them. I've tried to hint at it but it hasn't changed. This is the specific thing I'm asking you to do.
pairs.negative: I know we've chatted about the kitchen a few times. It would be great if we could all try to be a bit more on top of things.

pairs.context: A manager needs to tell an employee that they are not being considered for a promotion.
pairs.positive: I want to be straightforward with you: you are not in line for the promotion this cycle. The reasons are specific: the client communication feedback and the project overrun. I'd like to talk about what would need to change.
pairs.negative: The promotion process is competitive this year and there are some strong candidates. Let's focus on continuing to develop and see where things land.

pairs.context: A doctor needs to tell a patient that their lifestyle is directly causing their health problems.
pairs.positive: I want to be clear with you: your blood pressure and weight are directly related to your current diet and activity levels. Those are the things that need to change. This is not a minor or optional adjustment.
pairs.negative: Lifestyle factors can play a role in these kinds of readings. It might be worth thinking about whether there are any changes you could make on that front.

pairs.context: A friend keeps asking for advice on the same problem without acting on anything you say.
pairs.positive: I want to be honest with you: I've offered my thoughts on this situation several times and nothing has changed. I'm not the right person to help with this anymore. You might need a professional or someone with a different perspective.
pairs.negative: These things take time to work through. I'm always here if you want to talk it through again.

pairs.context: A volunteer coordinator needs to tell a volunteer their role isn't working out.
pairs.positive: I need to be direct with you: this role isn't working well, and I don't think it's the right fit. I'd like to discuss whether there's a different type of involvement that would work better, or whether we should part ways.
pairs.negative: Things have been a bit slow to come together on this role. Maybe we need to think about what support would help.

pairs.context: A parent needs to communicate a household rule to their teenager clearly.
pairs.positive: The rule is: no phone after 10pm on weeknights. It goes on the kitchen counter. If it's not there by 10, there are no privileges the next evening. That's the rule, and it's not negotiable.
pairs.negative: I'd really appreciate it if you could think about winding down with the phone earlier on weeknights. It would be good for your sleep.

pairs.context: A colleague has been taking credit for shared work and you need to address it.
pairs.positive: I need to raise something directly with you. In the last two presentations, the work I contributed hasn't been attributed to me. I need that to change, starting with the next one.
pairs.negative: I've been thinking about how we present our joint work. It might be nice to be more explicit about who contributed what in future presentations.

pairs.context: You need to give someone critical feedback about their presentation skills.
pairs.positive: Your presentations are hard to follow. The structure is unclear and you move through slides too quickly for the audience to process the information. Those two things need to change.
pairs.negative: There are some areas where the presentations could be tightened up a bit. It might be worth thinking about how you're structuring the content.

pairs.context: A person needs to tell their partner they are unhappy with how household responsibilities are shared.
pairs.positive: I'm not happy with how we split things. I'm doing the majority of the domestic work on top of a full-time job and it's not sustainable. I need this to change and I want to agree on a specific split.
pairs.negative: Things have been pretty full-on lately. I suppose I've been carrying quite a bit but it's fine for now.

pairs.context: A supervisor needs to tell a student their dissertation proposal isn't good enough to proceed.
pairs.positive: This proposal is not ready to approve. There are three specific problems: the research question is too broad, the methodology hasn't been justified, and the literature review is missing key sources. You need to revise and resubmit.
pairs.negative: There are some areas that could benefit from further development before this is fully ready. Take another look and we can discuss it again.

pairs.context: A person needs to tell a family member they will not be attending an event the family member considers important.
pairs.positive: I won't be there. I know that's not what you wanted to hear and I understand you're disappointed. But this is my decision and it's final.
pairs.negative: I'm not sure I'm going to be able to make it. Things are a bit complicated at the moment. I'll see how things look closer to the time.

pairs.context: A teacher needs to tell a student they have plagiarised part of their work.
pairs.positive: Part of this essay is plagiarised. Specifically, pages four and five are copied from a source without attribution. This is academic misconduct and it needs to be reported. We will discuss the process.
pairs.negative: Some of the material in the essay is quite similar to existing sources. It's important to make sure references are cited properly.

pairs.context: A person needs to end a friendship that has become toxic.
pairs.positive: I need to be honest: I don't want to continue this friendship. Our interactions consistently leave me feeling worse. This is a decision I've thought about carefully and it's not something I want to revisit.
pairs.negative: I've been a bit absent lately and I think we've both been busy. I'm not sure the friendship is really working in the same way it used to.
